title: P&C Transactional Data — Staff & Volume by Client/Function (Pivot)
grain: one row per pivot label — a client, or a function/sub-function nested under a client — with summed Total Staff and Average Monthly Volume
summary: A hierarchical pivot summarizing the Data Collection sheet. Row Labels interleave client names with the functions and sub-functions beneath each client, and each row carries the summed Total Staff and summed Average Monthly Volume for that label. It is a quick roll-up of staffing and throughput by client and by function.
use_cases: What is a client's total staff and monthly transaction volume? (lookup)
Which clients have the highest total staff or volume? (ranking)
How is a client's staff/volume distributed across functions and sub-functions? (segment)
Total staff or volume aggregated across all clients (aggregation)

columns.name: Row Labels
columns.note: mixed hierarchy: client names at the top level, function/sub-function names indented beneath

columns.name: Sum of Total Staff
columns.note: summed total staff for the label

columns.name: Sum of Average Monthly Volume
columns.note: summed average monthly transaction volume for the label

not_answerable.q: What is the pricing or revenue per client?
not_answerable.reason: the pivot summarizes only Total Staff and Average Monthly Volume; no pricing/financial measure is included

not_answerable.q: What are the task-level descriptions or SLAs?
not_answerable.reason: this is an aggregated roll-up; task/SLA detail lives in the 2. Data Collection sheet

not_answerable.q: How does staff/volume trend over time?
not_answerable.reason: single Sep-2025 snapshot with no time dimension

warnings: Row Labels mixes two grain levels (clients and their functions/sub-functions); rows are not a single flat entity list
Sum of Total Staff repeats each client's total on the client row and again across its function rows (hierarchical subtotals)
